assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The intragroup dividend gives rise to a recognised intragroup receivable/payable before elimination on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency-denominated receivable is a potential hedged item within the general IFRS 9 hedge accounting model, subject to consolidation-level restrictions on intragroup items.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately recognises cash flow hedging, including for highly probable forecast transactions, making it a distinct peer hedge accounting model relevant to foreign currency exposures.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 treat hedges of foreign currency risk on net investments in foreign operations as a separate hedge accounting model in consolidated financial statements.
